raw_consumer_statement:
I have opened, and AMEX have closed a dispute multiple times regarding a purchase at XXXX XXXX XXXX. Most recently I was asked for a complete timeline of what happened and I provided this but the dispute was then closed again stating that the timeline to make a return had passed. Makes no sense. 

The return period was in XXXX and I attempted to return in XX/XX/year> and you have all the supporting documentation. I was entitled to return the product and refused by the store. This is the perfect scenario for a dispute, but because it is XXXX you do not care and prefer to help XXXX. 
Customer buys something. Customer is entitled to return. Store refuses return in contradiction to their policy and XXXX XXXX law. Customer opens dispute and should receive a dispute. 

Charge XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX, 2023 {$300.00} XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, XXXX XXXX, XXXX Card Member : XXXX XXXX
